Title: Haplotype-phased genome unveils the butylphthalide biosynthesis and homoploid hybrid origin of Ligusticum chuanxiong

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phthalide tailoring and interconversion; C-4/C-5 desaturation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms related target products and promotes formation of target product precursor/product pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Promotes C-4/C-5 desaturation of senkyunolide A to form (S)-3-n-butylphthalide and of ligustilide to form butylidenephthalide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: First-line drug/active medicinal compound for ischemic stroke therapy
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: E. coli feeding assay; in vitro recombinant enzyme assay; GC-MS metabolite profiling; enzyme kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lc2OGD1 significantly promoted conversion of senkyunolide A to (S)-3-n-butylphthalide and ligustilide to butylidenephthalide in feeding assays. 2. In vitro enzyme assays showed significantly higher butylidenephthalide production than control. 3. It was the best-performing 2OGD for butylidenephthalide production among tested 2OGDs.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizome tissue context; heterologous feeding with senkyunolide A or ligustilide
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homoeologous allele pair with Lc2OGD2 on chromosome 6; one of six identified P4,5Ds; activity may be partly obscured by spontaneous reactions; relatively weak enzyme activity overall for P4,5Ds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for metabolic engineering or up-regulation to enhance (S)-3-n-butylphthalide production; potentially useful in gene stacking with other P4,5Ds
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; multiple-tissue RNA-seq; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pMAL vector system in E. coli

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phthalide tailoring and interconversion; C-4/C-5 desaturation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and related branch product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Promotes C-4/C-5 desaturation converting senkyunolide A to (S)-3-n-butylphthalide and ligustilide to butylidenephthalide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: First-line drug/active medicinal compound for ischemic stroke therapy
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: E. coli feeding assay; in vitro recombinant enzyme assay; GC-MS metabolite profiling; enzyme kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lc2OGD2 significantly promoted both investigated phthalide conversions in feeding assays. 2. In vitro enzyme assays supported significant production of (S)-3-n-butylphthalide and butylidenephthalide. 3. It was the most efficient 2OGD for (S)-3-n-butylphthalide formation with kcat/Km = 0.38 μM^-1 min^-1. 4. It was considered among the most reliable P4,5Ds.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes formation of (S)-3-n-butylphthalide and butylidenephthalide
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizome tissue context; heterologous feeding with senkyunolide A or ligustilide
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homoeologous allele of Lc2OGD1 on chromosome 6; among the most reliable P4,5Ds validated by both assay systems
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Top candidate for metabolic engineering/up-regulation to improve (S)-3-n-butylphthalide yield
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; multiple-tissue RNA-seq; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pMAL vector system in E. coli

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phthalide tailoring and interconversion; C-4/C-5 desaturation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and related branch product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Promotes C-4/C-5 desaturation converting senkyunolide A to (S)-3-n-butylphthalide and ligustilide to butylidenephthalide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: First-line drug/active medicinal compound for ischemic stroke therapy
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: E. coli feeding assay; in vitro recombinant enzyme assay; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lc2OGD3 significantly promoted both investigated phthalide conversions in feeding assays. 2. In vitro enzyme assays supported significantly higher (S)-3-n-butylphthalide and butylidenephthalide production than control. 3. It was considered one of the most reliable P4,5Ds.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizome tissue context; heterologous feeding with senkyunolide A or ligustilide
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located on Chr2B; among the most reliable P4,5Ds validated by both feeding and in vitro assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for metabolic engineering and gene stacking to increase (S)-3-n-butylphthalide
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; multiple-tissue RNA-seq; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pMAL vector system in E. coli

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phthalide tailoring and interconversion; C-4/C-5 desaturation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and related branch product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Promotes C-4/C-5 desaturation converting senkyunolide A to (S)-3-n-butylphthalide and ligustilide to butylidenephthalide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: First-line drug/active medicinal compound for ischemic stroke therapy
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: E. coli feeding assay; in vitro recombinant enzyme assay; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lc2OGD4 significantly promoted both investigated conversions in feeding assays. 2. In vitro assays supported significantly increased butylidenephthalide production relative to control. 3. It is an independently recruited P4,5D from the DOXC30 clade.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizome tissue context; heterologous feeding with senkyunolide A or ligustilide
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located on Chr7B; phylogenetically distinct from Lc2OGD1-3, suggesting independent recruitment of desaturase activity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Additional gene for combinatorial engineering of phthalide production
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; multiple-tissue RNA-seq; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pMAL vector system in E. coli

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Ligusticum chuanxiong; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Ligusticum chuanxiong; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phthalide tailoring and interconversion; C-4/C-5 desaturation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and related branch product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: CYP-mediated C-4/C-5 desaturation converting senkyunolide A to (S)-3-n-butylphthalide and ligustilide to butylidenephthalide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: First-line drug/active medicinal compound for ischemic stroke therapy
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Nicotiana benthamiana transient feeding assay; yeast microsomal enzyme assay; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LcCYP72A1132 significantly promoted conversion of senkyunolide A to (S)-3-n-butylphthalide and ligustilide to butylidenephthalide in feeding assays. 2. In vitro yeast microsomal assays confirmed significant production of both products. 3. It was considered one of the most reliable P4,5Ds.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizome tissue context; transient expression in N. benthamiana; yeast microsomal assay with NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of two CYP-derived P4,5Ds; CYPs showed different substrate/conversion preferences
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising candidate for metabolic engineering of (S)-3-n-butylphthalide biosynthesis in heterologous systems or L. chuanxiong
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; multiple-tissue RNA-seq; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pSuper1300 transient expression in N. benthamiana; pESC-Ura/galactose-inducible expression in yeast

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Ligusticum chuanxiong; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Ligusticum chuanxiong; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Phthalide tailoring and interconversion; C-4/C-5 desaturation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and related branch product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: CYP-mediated C-4/C-5 desaturation converting senkyunolide A to (S)-3-n-butylphthalide and ligustilide to butylidenephthalide
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: First-line drug/active medicinal compound for ischemic stroke therapy
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Nicotiana benthamiana transient feeding assay; yeast microsomal enzyme assay; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LcCYP716E110 significantly promoted the two investigated phthalide conversions in feeding assays. 2. In vitro yeast microsomal assays confirmed significant production of both products. 3. It was considered one of the most reliable P4,5Ds. 4. It belongs to an expanded CYP716E tandem cluster in L. chuanxiong.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizome tissue context; transient expression in N. benthamiana; yeast microsomal assay with NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located in a CYP716E tandem duplication region strongly expanded in L. chuanxiong, especially in LcHapB; nearby paralogs may have similar catalytic functions but remain untested
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising candidate for engineering enhanced (S)-3-n-butylphthalide production; tandem-cluster paralogs may provide additional engineering resources
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; multiple-tissue RNA-seq; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pSuper1300 transient expression in N. benthamiana; pESC-Ura/galactose-inducible expression in yeast
